codeReview.summary: Fail. The fix resolves the original nested clear/null-emission problem and keeps the exercised nested-update paths normalized, but the changed merge/normalization logic still does not satisfy the mixed flat/nested parity contract: once settings have been normalized into the persisted `audio.*` shape, later flat-only alias updates can be silently ignored.

issues.description: `RealtimeSessionModelSettings::merge()` keeps the prior nested `audio` subtree when an update only uses flat aliases (`audio` is preserved from the existing state at lines 400-404), and `normalize_effective()` then always prefers the nested values over the flat aliases (`input.*` at lines 491-538 and `output.*` at lines 547-574, especially `output.voice.clone().or_else(|| normalized.voice.clone())`). Because both `RealtimeSession::update_agent()` and the websocket/SIP model `update_session()` paths persist normalized settings via `without_clear_markers()`, the stored state usually contains both representations. A later flat-only update like `{ voice: Some("verse") }`, `{ speed: Some(0.8) }`, or `{ output_audio_format: ... }` therefore loses to the stale nested value and never becomes the effective payload/state. That still violates the feature contract's requirement that mixed flat and nested audio-setting updates normalize to the caller's intended effective settings.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The `rust-runtime-worker` skill should explicitly warn that `cargo test ... -- --exact` can exit 0 when no tests match, because the worker called out that nuance as the main procedure gap worth documenting.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: In `/Users/staticpayload/.factory/missions/a79cf586-bde6-417e-92a4-dc7681913eac/handoffs/2026-04-16T16-44-48-930Z__realtime-session-and-transport-parity__50de2da9-90da-493c-9729-f5865c644ecb.json`, `handoff.skillFeedback.suggestedChanges[0]` says the skill should note that a missing `cargo test -- --exact` match exits 0 with zero tests.

summary: Re-reviewed the fixed feature against the prior failing scrutiny report, mission contract, latest worker handoff/transcript skeleton, skill guidance, and commit `7c4269b606150b3c33cc033f04f562a5e4fe0cbc`. The previous blocking issue around nested clear/null emission is fixed, but the implementation still fails the mixed flat/nested update contract because normalized persisted audio state overrides later flat-only alias updates, so the feature should remain in failing scrutiny status.
